# type-vocabulary.tsv — canonical owners for cross-crate Rust types.
#
# Format:
#   <name> <kind> <owner-path> <mode> <notes>
#
# mode:
#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path
#   audit    audit reports it, but changed-file hook does not fail
#
# Keep this list small and intentional. It is not every Rust type in the
# workspace; it is the cross-cutting vocabulary whose duplication fragments the
# architecture.

LuaState        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical interpreter state
GlobalState     struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical shared VM state
CallInfo        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical call frame record
LuaValue        enum    crates/lua-types/src/value.rs    enforce  canonical TValue replacement
LuaError        enum    crates/lua-types/src/error.rs    enforce  canonical error type
LuaString       struct  crates/lua-types/src/string.rs   audit    currently duplicated in lua-vm; reconcile before enforce
LuaTable        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/table.rs       audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaProto        struct  crates/lua-types/src/proto.rs    audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaClosure      enum    crates/lua-types/src/closure.rs  audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
UpVal           enum    crates/lua-types/src/upval.rs    audit    canonical upvalue shape
LuaUserData     struct  crates/lua-types/src/userdata.rs enforce  canonical userdata type
LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum
StackIdx        struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical stack index
CallInfoIdx     struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical call-info index
OpCode          enum    crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
Instruction     struct  crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce

1611	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1612	///
1613	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1614	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1615	    state: &mut LuaState,
1616	    z: ZIO,
1617	    name: &[u8],
1618	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1619	) -> LuaStatus {
1620	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1621	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1622	    state.inc_nny();
1623	
1624	    let mut p = SParser {
1625	        z,
1626	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1627	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1628	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1629	        name: name.to_vec(),
1630	    };
1631	
1632	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1633	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1634	
1635	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1636	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1637	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1638	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1639	
1640	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1641	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1642	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1643	
1644	    // C: decnny(L)
1645	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1646	    state.dec_nny();
1647	
1648	    status
1649	}
1650

1652	// PORT STATUS
1653	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)

1659	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1660	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1661	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1662	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1663	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1664	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1665	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1666	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,
1667	//                  set_ci*, next_ci, etc.) are best-guess stubs to be wired
1668	//                  up in Phase B once the LuaState API is finalised.
1669	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1670

555	impl Collectable for LuaState {}
556	
557	// ─── GlobalState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
558	
559	/// Function-pointer signature for the text-source parser, installed on
560	/// [`GlobalState::parser_hook`] by the embedder.
561	///
562	/// The implementation lives in `lua-parse`; `lua-vm` cannot depend on it
563	/// directly (that would form a cycle), so the parser is reached via this
564	/// function pointer registered at startup.
565	pub type ParserHook = fn(
566	    state: &mut LuaState,
567	    name: &[u8],
568	    firstchar: i32,
569	) -> Result<GcRef<lua_types::closure::LuaLClosure>, LuaError>;
570	
571	/// Process-wide state shared by all Lua threads.
572	///
573	/// C: `global_State` in `lstate.h`.
574	/// types.tsv: `global_State → GlobalState`
575	///
576	/// Not exposed directly at the API; accessed via `state.global()` / `state.global_mut()`.
577	pub struct GlobalState {
578	    /// Phase-B hook for the Lua text parser. Set by the embedder (`lua-cli`
579	    /// or stdlib host) to bridge the cyclic crate split between `lua-vm` and
580	    /// `lua-parse`: when `f_parser` decides the chunk is text, it invokes
581	    /// this hook instead of the parser stub. `None` leaves the stub in place
582	    /// so unit tests that never load text still work.
583	    pub parser_hook: Option<ParserHook>,
584	
585	    // C: l_mem totalbytes — Phase D memory accounting
586	    // types.tsv: global_State.totalbytes → isize
587	    pub totalbytes: isize,
588	
589	    // C: l_mem GCdebt — Phase D GC pacing

1015	fn get_f_reader() -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1016	    // TODO(port): real implementation reads from a File handle.
1017	    move || None
1018	}
1019	
1020	/// Internal chunk reader that returns a single buffer slice then signals EOF.
1021	///
1022	/// C: `static const char *getS(lua_State *L, void *ud, size_t *size)`
1023	fn make_string_reader(data: Vec<u8>) -> impl FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
1024	    let mut remaining = Some(data);
1025	    move || remaining.take()
1026	}
1027	
1028	/// Load a file as a Lua chunk. Returns `LUA_OK` on success or an error code.
1029	///
1030	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadfilex(lua_State *L, const char *filename, const char *mode)`
1031	// TODO(port): uses std::fs which is banned outside lua-cli; stub for Phase A.
1032	pub fn load_filex(
1033	    state: &mut LuaState,
1034	    filename: Option<&[u8]>,
1035	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1036	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1037	    // TODO(port): open file with std::fs::File, handle BOM, skip shebang line.
1038	    // For Phase A we always fail cleanly.
1039	    let _ = (filename, mode);
1040	    Err(LuaError::runtime(format_args!(
1041	        "luaL_loadfilex not implemented in Phase A (std::fs banned outside lua-cli)"
1042	    )))
1043	}
1044	
1045	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk.
1046	///
1047	/// C: `LUALIB_API int luaL_loadbufferx(lua_State *L, const char *buff, size_t size, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1048	pub fn load_bufferx(
1049	    state: &mut LuaState,
1050	    buff: &[u8],
1051	    name: &[u8],
1052	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1053	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1054	    // C: LoadS ls; ls.s = buff; ls.size = size;
1055	    // C: return lua_load(L, getS, &ls, name, mode);
1056	    // TODO(phase-b): state.load expects (chunk: &[u8], name, mode) in state_stub; the reader-based loader needs a load_with_reader API match.
1057	    let _reader = make_string_reader(buff.to_vec());
1058	    let ok = state.load(buff, name, mode)?;
1059	    Ok(if ok { 0 } else { 1 })
1060	}
1061	
1062	/// Load a buffer as a Lua chunk (no mode argument).
1063	///
1064	/// C: `#define luaL_loadbuffer(L,s,sz,n) luaL_loadbufferx(L,s,sz,n,NULL)`
1065	pub fn load_buffer(
1066	    state: &mut LuaState,
1067	    buff: &[u8],
1068	    name: &[u8],
1069	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1070	    load_bufferx(state, buff, name, None)
1071	}
1072	
1073	/// Load a NUL-terminated byte-string as a Lua chunk.
1074	///
